howItWorks.intro: This work-life balance calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is good work-life balance?
faq.answer: There's no universal standard, but key indicators include: working 40-45 hours/week, exercising 3+ times/week, getting 7+ hours sleep, having meaningful social connections, and using your vacation time. The right balance is personal.

faq.question: How many hours is too many to work?
faq.answer: Research shows productivity drops significantly after 50 hours/week and collapses after 55. The WHO found working 55+ hours/week increases stroke risk by 35% and heart disease by 17%. Sustainable high performance requires recovery.

faq.question: How can I improve work-life balance?
faq.answer: Set firm boundaries (no email after 7pm), schedule personal time like meetings, take your vacation days, learn to say no, batch similar tasks, delegate, and remember that being busy isn't the same as being productive.
